subject: Sociology
title: Ritual and Social Cohesion
description: Learn Ritual and Social Cohesion in one minute.

hook.text: Every Sunday, millions sit in pews doing the same thing at the same time. Why does this feel powerful?

buildup.text: Durkheim called this collective effervescence — the energy that arises when people worship together.

discovery.text: Rituals don't just express beliefs. They generate feelings of unity and reinforce group identity.

twist.text: Secular rituals work the same way. Concerts, protests, and sports games all produce collective energy.

climax.text: Religion endures partly because rituals keep renewing the social bonds that hold communities together.

punchline.text: Worship builds bonds, not just faith.
